title: Session Log: VOLT OLT Reverse Engineering & Live Testing

steps: Identified all three executables as PyInstaller bundles (Python 3.9)
Discovered PyArmor v52.6.11 protection on all 18 app modules
Downloaded Python 3.9 64-bit embeddable to match pytransform.pyd ABI
Used sys.settrace() to hook into PyArmor runtime decryption
Captured 161 decrypted code objects with full symbol tables
Extracted ALL runtime enum values by importing com_def through pytransform

key_protocol_values_extracted.transport: Raw Ethernet via Scapy (Ether + LLC from scapy.layers.l2)

network_topology.description: Network path from Linux box to OLT SFP

evidence: It's a cAP device behind the ONU, reachable through the GPON link
The OLT SFP itself has NO IP address on the network

vlan_4091_setup.description: Created VLAN 4091 path for L2 management traffic

R01.vlan_1: untagged on all ports (preserves existing traffic)

SW02.vlan_4091: tagged on ether1, untagged on sfp-sfpplus1 (strips tag for OLT)

tests_performed: SNAP frames with various OUI/protocol IDs
Broadcast and unicast to OLT MAC
Both big-endian and little-endian magic code encoding
UDP scan on all ports 1-1023 + common high ports
TCP scan on 330 ports from same subnet
ARP probes on 14 common default subnets (192.168.1.x, 192.168.0.x, 10.0.0.x, etc.)
l2_probing_results.result: OLT SFP responds to nothing. Zero packets captured from the OLT on any interface.

phase_2_live_testing.hypothesis: The VOLT management protocol likely works at the SFP electrical interface level, not through the switch fabric. The management frames may need to be sent directly from a host NIC's SFP port to the OLT SFP, bypassing the switch entirely. The CRS354's switch ASIC (even with HW offload disabled and software bridging) may still not forward the specific frame type the OLT expects.

phase_2_5_vlan_4090_discovery.title: VLAN 4090 Discovery (Tibit MCMS)
findings: Juniper Unified PON / Tibit MCMS documentation revealed management VLAN is 4090 (not 4091)
The VOLT OLT SFP is likely a Tibit MicroPlug OLT clone
phase_2_5_vlan_4090_discovery.vlan_4090_setup: Configured VLAN 4090 across all devices (same pattern as 4091). Probed with IEEE 1904.2 frames — still no response through the multi-hop path.

routeros_upgrade_issue.action: Upgraded SW02 from RouterOS 7.11 to latest
routeros_upgrade_issue.problem: Broke sfp-sfpplus1 link — auto-negotiation behavior changed
routeros_upgrade_issue.result: Link restored: status: link-ok, 1Gbps full-duplex, sfp-rx-loss: no
routeros_upgrade_issue.important_note: The OLT SFP requires forced 1G-baseX, no auto-negotiation

results_after_link_restore: cAP at 192.168.10.19 not yet pingable (ONU re-registration pending)
VLAN 4090 probes: 0 responses from OLT through multi-hop path
1 captured packet was a Docker container ARP, not from OLT

phase_2_6_vendor_and_video_research.title: Vendor & Video Research

video_walkthrough_findings.description: 33-minute Spanish tutorial video of someone configuring the exact same SFP OLT in a MikroTik

findings.title: Discovery works through a MikroTik bridge
findings.detail: SFP port + Ethernet ports bridged, Windows PC on one of the bridged ports, VOLT tool discovers OLT through the bridge.

findings.title: Interface selection by NIC MAC address
findings.detail: Tool scans interfaces, user picks by MAC. Pure L2 discovery, no IP or VLAN needed.

findings.title: OLT TX enable is critical
findings.detail: Must be toggled in the software on first use before the OLT communicates with ONUs. Maps to our RE: OpticalTxEnable (Message ID 256).

findings.title: Works through a bridge, not direct
findings.detail: "The OLT doesn't necessarily have to show as connected to our computer" — the bridge forwards the management frames.

findings.title: Simple media converter works
findings.detail: No special host device, just L2 connectivity.

findings.title: PPPoE setup on MikroTik
findings.detail: Bridge created with SFP + Ethernet ports, PPPoE server on the bridge, NAT masquerade for internet, ONUs configured in PPPoE mode.

findings.title: ONU management via web UI
findings.detail: ONUs accessed at 192.168.1.1, configured for PPPoE.

findings.title: Real-time monitoring
findings.detail: Temperature, uptime, online/offline per ONU, remote activate/deactivate individual ONUs from the VOLT tool.

phase_2_6_vendor_and_video_research.key_insight_why_probes_failed: The video shows the PC on the same MikroTik as the SFP — one bridge, one device. Our setup has the Linux box going through SW01 → R01 → SW02 (three hops, two additional switch fabrics). Tomorrow's direct patch into SW02 replicates the video's topology.

phase_3_on_site_testing_day_2.title: On-Site Testing (Day 2)

tests_performed.config: Standard bridge, HW offload disabled, VLAN filtering off, isolated bridge (protocol-mode=none, only ether48 + sfp1)
Pure CPU bridging, no switch ASIC, sfp-sfpplus10 + sfp-sfpplus11, olt-mgmt bridge with protocol-mode=none, hw-offload=false

volt_pcapng.description: VOLT protocol packets captured from Windows VOLT tool

confirmed_findings: VOLT tool sends correctly formatted discovery frames
Only responses captured: CRS354 CDP/LLDP/MNDP — zero from OLT SFP

phase_3_on_site_testing_day_2.conclusion: Both VOLT-32 OLT SFP sticks are defective. They are optically and electrically alive (link-ok, normal power levels, valid EEPROM) but their embedded management firmware does not respond to discovery frames. Proven across five different host devices including the CCR2004 where no hardware filtering is possible. Replacement units ordered.

discovery_cycle.description: The VOLT tool sends continuous Get (byte8=0x00) requests in a repeating cycle
steps: OnuStatus queries (0x0201/0x0209) for each ONU slot 0-31
Cycle repeats with incrementing sequence numbers

items: Receive replacement OLT SFP sticks (1-2 days)
TP-Link MC220L media converter arriving for simplest test setup
Build Linux management tool using decoded protocol — ready to test immediately
Test FS.com GPON-SFP-OLT-MAC-I when it arrives (128 ONU, Class C+)

reverse_engineering_scripts.purpose: Full trace with class method extraction
Targeted olt_socket decryption with Crypto deps
Runtime bytecode dumper via exec hook
Scapy hook for live packet capture

network_probing_scripts.purpose: Extended probe with all EtherType combinations
UDP scan + raw AF_PACKET socket probe
IP probe across 14 default subnets + DHCP
VLAN 4091 configuration for R01 + SW02 + Linux

R01.vlan_1: untagged on all bridge ports + bridge interface

SW02.vlan_4091: tagged on ether1, untagged on sfp-sfpplus1
SW02.vlan_1: untagged on all 61 bridge ports + bridge interface

SW01.vlan_4091: added in VLANs tab, port2 + SFP1 as members

revert_commands.description: If needed, disable VLAN filtering on R01 and SW02
